page.title: Marbella Cocktail Mixing Yacht Experience: Shake It at Sea

data.meta_description: Book a cocktail mixing yacht experience Marbella on a luxury charter from Puerto Banús. Shake, stir & sip at sea with a pro mixologist. From €749/2h.
data.summary: Combine luxury yacht charter with a hands-on cocktail masterclass on the Costa del Sol. Our cocktail mixing yacht experience Marbella includes a professional mixologist, premium spirits, and stunning sea views from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Available on select yachts from Puerto Banús, including Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and Mangusta 80.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Tier A yachts) to €4,719 minimum 4h (Mangusta 80).
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Professional mixologist on board to guide you through 3–4 signature cocktails.
Customise your menu: choose from classic mojitos, margaritas, or a bespoke creation.
Max capacity varies by yacht: 9–12 guests depending on vessel.
Ideal for hen parties, birthdays, corporate events, or a unique date night.
All equipment and premium spirits provided – just bring your enthusiasm.

faq.q: What is included in the cocktail mixing yacht experience Marbella?
faq.a: Your charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). Additionally, a professional mixologist will guide you through making 3–4 cocktails using premium spirits, fresh juices, and garnishes. All equipment is provided.

faq.q: Which yachts can I book for a cocktail mixing experience?
faq.a: You can book the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) from €749 for 2 hours, or the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719 for 4 hours (includes a free jet ski). Other vessels are available on request – contact us via WhatsApp for details.

faq.q: Can I customise the cocktails we make?
faq.a: Absolutely. Before your charter, you can discuss preferences with our team. Choose from classics like mojitos, margaritas, or daiquiris, or request a bespoke creation. The mixologist will adapt to your taste, dietary restrictions, and the fresh ingredients available that day.

faq.q: How long does the cocktail mixing session last?
faq.a: The mixology session typically lasts 45–60 minutes, depending on the number of cocktails. The rest of your charter is free for swimming, sunbathing, or cruising. You can book from 2 hours (Tier A) to full-day charters (8 hours) to enjoy a relaxed pace.

faq.q: Is this experience suitable for beginners?
faq.a: Yes, it’s designed for all skill levels. The mixologist explains techniques step by step, from muddling to shaking and straining. You’ll learn pro tips and finish with a drink you made yourself. No prior experience needed – just a love for cocktails.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Safety is our priority. If the captain deems conditions unsafe, we’ll reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24–48 hours before departure if changes are needed.

faq.q: Can I bring my own drinks or food?
faq.a: Our charters already include a selection of drinks and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or drinks, but please note that glass bottles are not permitted on board for safety reasons. We recommend using plastic or cans.

faq.q: How do I book the cocktail mixing yacht experience?
faq.a: You can book directly via our website for Tier A yachts (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) or contact us on WhatsApp for other vessels. A 50% deposit secures your date, with the balance due 7 days before departure. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance.

data.body_html:
Imagine shaking a perfect margarita while the Mediterranean sparkles below you. Our cocktail mixing yacht experience Marbella turns that vision into reality. Depart from Puerto Banús on a private yacht equipped with a professional mixologist, premium spirits, and all the tools you need to craft Instagram-worthy cocktails. Whether you’re celebrating a hen do, a birthday, or simply want a fresh way to enjoy the coast, this charter blends hands-on fun with the luxury of a private boat. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, with all drinks, snacks, skipper, and VAT included.

Why Choose a Cocktail Mixing Yacht Experience in Marbella?

Marbella’s coastline is stunning from any angle, but a yacht adds a layer of exclusivity that a beach bar simply can’t match. With our mixology charter, you get a private floating cocktail lounge. The mixologist brings fresh juices, herbs, and premium spirits, and guides you through creating 3–4 signature drinks. You’ll learn proper shaking, muddling, and garnishing techniques – then enjoy your creations with views of La Concha mountain and the Golden Mile. It’s interactive, educational, and delicious.

Which Yachts Are Available for the Mixology Charter?

We offer this experience on several vessels from our fleet, all departing from Puerto Banús. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect for intimate sessions. Prices for these Tier A yachts are €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, or €2,299 for 8 hours. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge and a free jet ski – minimum 4 hours at €4,719. Other vessels like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Ferretti 94 are available on request; contact us via WhatsApp for custom quotes.

What’s Included in the Cocktail Mixing Package?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%). On the drinks front, you get unlimited soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, and cava, plus light snacks. The mixology add-on brings a professional bartender, a selection of premium spirits (vodka, gin, rum, tequila, triple sec), fresh citrus, herbs, syrups, and all bar tools. You’ll make 3–4 cocktails as a group, with the mixologist explaining each step. No hidden costs – the price you see is the price you pay.

Sample Itinerary for a 4-Hour Mixology Charter

  • Departure: 10:00 from Puerto Banús marina
  • Cruise: Head east towards Cabopino or west to Cala del Faro, passing Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile
  • Mixology session: 10:30–11:30 – learn to make a mojito, margarita, and a secret house cocktail
  • Free time: Swim stop at Cala Cortés or anchor near the dunes of Cabopino
  • Return: 14:00 to Puerto Banús, with a final cocktail in hand

Longer charters (6–8 hours) allow a lunch stop at a chiringuito or a visit to Estepona or Sotogrande.

Perfect for Celebrations and Corporate Events

Our cocktail mixing yacht experience Marbella is a hit for hen parties, birthday surprises, and team-building events. The interactive nature gets everyone involved, and the mixologist can tailor the drinks to your group’s preferences. For corporate clients, we can include branded cocktail napkins or a custom cocktail name. With a maximum of 12 guests on most yachts, the atmosphere remains intimate and exclusive. Contact us to discuss dietary requirements or special requests.

How to Book Your Cocktail Mixing Yacht Charter

Booking is straightforward. For Tier A yachts (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can reserve directly on our website with a 50% deposit. For all other vessels, message us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and any custom requests. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance, especially during peak season (June–September). Once confirmed, you’ll receive a full itinerary and prep checklist. Browse all Marbella yacht charters to see the full fleet.

Yes, it’s designed for all skill levels. The mixologist explains techniques step by step, from muddling to shaking and straining. You’ll learn pro tips and finish with a drink you made yourself. No prior experience needed – just a love for cocktails.

Safety is our priority. If the captain deems conditions unsafe, we’ll reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely and will contact you 24–48 hours before departure if changes are needed.

Our charters already include a selection of drinks and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or drinks, but please note that glass bottles are not permitted on board for safety reasons. We recommend using plastic or cans.
